raw_consumer_statement:
On XXXX I was sent a text message from XXXX ( XXXX ) XXXX that said, " Purchase of {$520.00} was attempted at XXXX, XXXX on your CitiAccount. If this was you, no action needed. If not, XXXX. '' I immediately called the number as I was traveling across the US and had been using my card at hotels and gas stations along the way and thought it was from that. 

The person answered ( XXXX ), and he sounded like a typical call center agent from XXXX. He said that my card had XXXX charges including a XXXX XXXX ticket for {$1000.00} ( not exact ), a game company XXXX? that had a {$500.00} ( also not exact ) plus XXXX other charges ( I can not exactly remember them ). He asked me if they were my charges. I said no. He then requested me to verify : 1. My address, 2. The last XXXX numbers of my credit card, and 3. The last 4 of my SSN. 

I provided all this information. He then said he would reverse the charges and put a security hold on my card. He then offered to close my card and send me a new one. For some reason, I apologized and said that I preferred to call back to get that done. 

When I called the credit card company, they shared that I had no " fraudulant '' charges on my account and none had been flagged NOR had they called me. They suggested I contact FTC.gov to report this transaction ( since my SSN had been partially shared ), and I did close my credit card with that account.
